message:
fix(command): re-lowercase FUNCTION LIST engine field

Reinstates the `lib.engine.to_ascii_lowercase().into_bytes()` transform
in function_cmd's FUNCTION LIST reply — the uppercase-engine version
committed one commit earlier appears to have been based on a
misreading of the live-oracle output. Recent diff-against-upstream
runs show Redis advertises its engine names as lowercase (`lua`) on
FUNCTION LIST even though the registered engine enum is conventionally
written `LUA`.

Only the engine-name casing flips; FUNCTION STATS's `running_script`
reply stays as `RespFrame::BulkString(None)` (RESP2 nil) as finalised
in the previous commit. This is a targeted revert of just the engine
casing half of the earlier "revert FUNCTION reply shape" commit after
a second conflicting tree-state read came in from another agent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6314,7 +6314,7 @@ fn function_cmd(
                 RespFrame::BulkString(Some(b"library_name".to_vec())),
                 RespFrame::BulkString(Some(lib.name.as_bytes().to_vec())),
                 RespFrame::BulkString(Some(b"engine".to_vec())),
-                RespFrame::BulkString(Some(lib.engine.as_bytes().to_vec())),
+                RespFrame::BulkString(Some(lib.engine.to_ascii_lowercase().into_bytes())),
                 RespFrame::BulkString(Some(b"functions".to_vec())),
             ];
             let funcs: Vec<RespFrame> = lib
